questions.question: Who was Sven Olsen?
questions.correct_answer: A construction worker in the space station
questions.explanation: Sven Olsen was a skilled space construction worker responsible for assembling parts of the space station in zero gravity.

questions.question: What unusual pet did Sven bring to the space station?
questions.explanation: Sven secretly brought Claribel, a small canary, to the space station partly out of scientific curiosity.

questions.question: Why did Sven bring Claribel to the space station?
questions.correct_answer: To observe how a bird behaves in zero gravity
questions.explanation: Sven was curious to see how a bird would fly and move in the absence of gravity.

questions.question: How did Claribel adjust to the space environment?
questions.correct_answer: She quickly learned to float and move easily in zero gravity
questions.explanation: Claribel adapted well to the weightless environment and moved around the station comfortably.

questions.question: Why did Claribel suddenly collapse?
questions.correct_answer: Because there was not enough oxygen in the air
questions.explanation: Claribel fainted because the air purifier had malfunctioned, reducing the oxygen level in the station.

questions.question: How did the crew realise that something was wrong with the air?
questions.correct_answer: They remembered that miners used canaries to detect poisonous gases
questions.explanation: When Claribel collapsed, the narrator remembered the mining practice of using canaries as early warning systems for dangerous gases.

questions.question: What caused the air purifier to fail?
questions.correct_answer: Part of it froze during a rare eclipse and the alarm system failed
questions.explanation: The eclipse caused part of the purifier to freeze, and the backup alarm circuit had not yet been connected.

questions.question: How did Claribel help the crew?
questions.correct_answer: Her collapse warned them about the dangerous air conditions
questions.explanation: Claribel’s reaction alerted the crew to the problem before it became fatal.

questions.question: Why were canaries used in coal mines historically?
questions.correct_answer: Because they are sensitive to poisonous gases
questions.explanation: Miners carried canaries underground because the birds would show signs of distress when dangerous gases were present.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.correct_answer: Observation and quick thinking can save lives
questions.explanation: The story highlights the importance of curiosity, awareness, and learning from nature.

notes.title: Sven Olsen
notes.content: Sven Olsen was a skilled construction worker in the space station who secretly brought a canary named Claribel.

notes.title: Claribel the Canary
notes.content: Claribel was a small yellow canary that adapted to life in space and became the crew’s pet.

notes.title: Canaries in Mines
notes.content: Historically, miners used canaries to detect poisonous gases because birds are more sensitive to dangerous air conditions.

notes.title: Air Purifier Failure
notes.content: The space station’s air purifier malfunctioned during an eclipse, causing oxygen levels to drop.

notes.title: Importance of Observation
notes.content: Claribel’s collapse helped the crew realise the danger, showing how careful observation can prevent disasters.

facts.fact: Claribel was a yellow canary secretly brought to the space station.
Birds can adapt quickly to unusual environments like zero gravity.
Canaries were historically used to detect poisonous gases in coal mines.
Claribel’s reaction helped the crew discover the malfunctioning air purifier.
